greetingDefault: Time is firebirds, stranger. State your business.
greetingFriendly: Ah — there's a face I trust. Or trust enough. What can the League do for you?
greetingHostile: I've heard what you did. Don't waste my time pretending it didn't happen.
farewellDefault: Safe travels. And remember — the League remembers its friends.
unknownKeywordResponse: That's outside my area of expertise. Ask me about trade, the League, or what's moving through this port.

keywords.response: Orlan. Guildmaster Factor for this sector. I speak for the Merchant League on Aragon. Which means I speak for commerce, and commerce speaks for everyone, whether they admit it or not.
I keep this port profitable. Ships come, ships go, cargo moves, credits flow. When something disrupts that — bandits, Church embargoes, noble squabbles — I find someone to fix it.

The Merchant League keeps the Known Worlds fed, clothed, and armed. The nobles fight their wars, the Church saves souls — the League makes sure everyone has enough to eat while they do it. We are not loved, but we are needed.
What do you need to move? I have supply lines to six jump gates from here. Legitimate cargo, obviously. For the other kind, talk to Yara in the lower city — I don't know a Yara, officially.

I have a shipment stuck at Bay 7. The dockmaster won't clear it without an inspection seal from the Church. Harkan's holding it up deliberately — politics. I need someone to... persuade the inspector that the seal was already processed.
That word doesn't exist in my vocabulary. I move goods. Some goods move through unconventional channels. The distinction matters enormously in a court of law.

Harkan is a problem. He has extended jurisdiction and a nose for anything that benefits the League. Officially, he investigates tech heresy. Unofficially, he is the Noble Houses' pressure valve on our operations.

Firebirds are the currency of the Known Worlds. Issued by the League, backed by guild reserves. The nobles call it a symbol of our overreach. We call it a symbol of their dependence. Funny how that works.

Yara keeps a stall in the lower quarter that sells 'curiosities.' Officially, I have no relationship with that establishment. Unofficially — tell her Orlan sends his regards and that the blue lamp is lit.

Aragon is a second-tier colony — never quite important enough to fight over, never obscure enough to be left alone. Which makes it interesting to those of us who know how to read a map. Something is stirring here.
Safe travels. The League's door is always open — to those who can afford the door.
